reason: Backend modules for agents, services, tools, models, and API must follow existing layout.
Family planning must use structured recoverable errors and graph-state routing.
LLM changes must preserve provider fallback contract and single invoke_with_fallback entry point.
Python typing, pytest, and service/API separation requirements.
New family scoring and fallback decisions should be structured logged.
Showcase/mock data should stay lightweight and JSON/static where needed.
Frontend family cards and state changes should stay in established component/hook layout.
New UI components must follow function component, Tailwind, and accessibility conventions.
Streaming state changes belong in useChat rather than page components.
Backend remains source of truth; frontend displays streamed state without global stores.
New API response fields need explicit TypeScript types.
Frontend lint/type quality bar for changed UI.
Feature changes backend state, SSE API payloads, frontend types, and UI display.
Avoid duplicating existing plan card, chat stream, tool, and spatial helper patterns.
Second-round family quality gate, rhythm scheduling, diet risk, evidence binding.
Risk-tiered family assurance display with folded evidence and fallback details.
Route-first map display with candidate POI toggle and weakened isochrone.
AMap POI provenance fields: source, trust_level, typecode, tags.
Propagate POI provenance and provide curated family fallback candidate.
Frontend contract for POI provenance and trust fields.
